title: Acronia vieirae
intro: Acronia vieirae, also known as Vieira's Acronia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Acronia vieirae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Acronia vieirae is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and can also be found in capsule or tablet form, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.

This family includes over 13,000 species of plants, many of which are known for their medicinal and ornamental properties.
